rob-ickes-trey-hensley.name: Rob Ickes & Trey Hensley
rob-ickes-trey-hensley.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Take a 15-time IBMA (International Bluegrass Music Association) Dobro Player of the Year and a Tennessee-born guitar prodigy who made his Grand Ole Opry debut at the age of 11, and you have Rob Ickes and Trey Hensley, a powerhouse acoustic duo that has electrified the acoustic music scene. Known for their white-hot picking, stone country vocals with soul and world class live musicianship, Ickes and Hensley meld together blues, bluegrass, country, rock and other string band music of all kinds to form a signature blend of music that defies restrictions of genre.

rockin-dopsie-jr-the-zydeco-twisters.name: Rockin' Dopsie Jr & The Zydeco Twisters
rockin-dopsie-jr-the-zydeco-twisters.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Schedule is subject to change

Rockin' Dopsie, Jr. & The Zydeco Twisters, are referred to as "Zydeco's Royal Family". For seven decades the high-energy stage name Rockin’ Dopsie (pronounced Doopsie) has stood tall in the deep-groove world of zydeco. In addition to his famous footwork, Dopsie’s performance presence is further fueled by his fleet-fingered playing of the rub-board. Dopsie’s percussive prowess both pushes and is propelled by the ferocious grooves fired up by the Zydeco Twisters.

school-of-song-caylie-g-mitch-gorman-pau.name: School of Song - Caylie G, Mitch Gorman, Paul Cournoyer & The Western Thistles
school-of-song-caylie-g-mitch-gorman-pau.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Days of Sessions: Aug 6-7

Schedule is subject to change

Caylie G - Caylie G is a queer Pop-Folk artist based in Edmonton, on Treaty Six Territory. With roots firmly planted in their prairie upbringings and a soft spot for glittering theatrics, Caylie has cultivated a sound best described as sparkly cowboy-pop. Their music explores the valleys and swells of love, heartbreak, and the complexities of being human, with a tender balance of vulnerability and strength. Caylie’s lyrics resonate with folks from all walks of life, capturing the essence of the human experience with softness and candour.


Mitch Gorman - Mitch Gorman is an emerging indie-folk singer-songwriter from Grand Prairie, Alberta. Known for his heartfelt lyrics and captivating melodies, Mitch draws inspiration from a blend of folk and alternative influences, channeling the essence of artists like Noah Kahan, Bon Iver, and Gregory Alan Isakov. Several of his other TikTok videos have reached over 500K+ views, demonstrating his growing popularity and ability to resonate with listeners.


Paul Cournoyer - A modern poet with a soft, clear voice, singer-songwriter Paul Cournoyer sets images dancing on musical waves that soothe and carry us away. A harmonious blend of rock and folk, his music is enveloped in a generous layer of refined pop, with well-placed nuances between emptiness and fullness. His accolades include, winner of the 2012 Chant'Ouest competition, quarter-finalist at the Granby International Song Festival, multiple nominations at the 2021 Trille Or Awards, and Breakout West Award nomination for Francophone Recording of the Year.


The Western Thistles - Blending old-time roots with modern-day storytelling, The Western Thistles are an Edmonton folk duo known for their clawhammer banjo, rhythm guitar, and rich vocal harmonies. Their sound draws from folk, roots, and old-time traditions while carving out a space distinctly their own. In 2025, The Western Thistles released their self-titled debut album, which reached #26 on CKUA’s Top 100 most-played albums of the year, standing as a testament to their commitment to crafting songs with heart, grit, and intention.

sister-ray.name: Sister Ray
sister-ray.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

At some point you confront the fact that you’ve evolved into the person you’ll be forever. Believer, the sophomore effort by Sister Ray, is an expansive folk project led by Toronto-based singer-songwriter Ella Coyes. They evaluate what it means to hold your centre while continually reappraising how to make sense of the world. Conjuring the barbed alternative folk and sweeping Americana of Tom Waits, Smog, and Lucinda Williams, the album unfurls and claws towards a greater emotional precision.

st-paul-the-broken-bones.name: St. Paul & The Broken Bones
st-paul-the-broken-bones.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Schedule is subject to change

St. Paul & The Broken Bones are a powerhouse soul ensemble from Alabama known for their electrifying live shows and impassioned vocals. The group has shared stages with The Rolling Stones and Black Pumas, and has performed at major festivals like Coachella, Glastonbury, Lollapalooza, and Bonnaroo. Their new self-titled sixth album marks a creative renewal, melding the band’s adventurous spirit with a return to soulful, song-focused roots.

supalung.name: SupaLung
supalung.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

SupaLung, a name paying homage to late-'60s rock powerhouse Terry Reid, affectionately known as “Superlungs”, is a firecracker four-piece that’s quickly becoming essential listening for fans of live alt-indie rock who celebrate depth, joy and groove. Fronted by the hauntingly earnest vocals and sharp songwriting of Sam Brookes, and co-piloted by multi-instrumentalist and producer Pete Josef, SupaLung is not just a band, it’s a moment, a feeling, a sonic hug with the heart of a drum that kicks.

syml.name: SYML
syml.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Days of Sessions: Aug 7

Schedule is subject to change

SYML is the solo venture of artist Brian Fennell. Welsh for “simple”, SYML makes music that taps into the instincts that drive us to places of sanctuary. His self-titled first release, included the multiplatinum song, “Where’s My Love” and the Gold Single fan favourite, “Girl." His sophomore album, The Day My Father Died, was described by NPR as “euphoric,” and featured collaborations with Lucius, Sara Watkins, and Charlotte Lawrence. SYML is also known for his presence across genres, including a feature on Lana Del Rey’s song “Paris, Texas,”, and contributions to songs by the UK-electronic artist George Fitzgerald and Colombian artist Elsa Y Elmar.

the-brudi-brothers.name: The Brudi Brothers
the-brudi-brothers.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

The Brudi Brothers are a dynamic band hailing from Seattle, who have an inimitable musical and performance dynamic replete with wit and humour. Often recognized as the city's favourite buskers and street poets, they are known for their raucous performances and distinctive old-world soul that stands out in the sphere of modern music. Alongside a touring résumé that includes support for Sierra Ferrell, The Head and the Heart, and Charley Crockett, they gained rapid popularity worldwide when a live clip of their 2025 single, "Me More Cowboy Than You," went viral, garnering over 60 million streams.

the-mbira-renaissance-band.name: The Mbira Renaissance Band
the-mbira-renaissance-band.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Schedule is subject to change

The Mbira Renaissance Band is one of Edmonton's most exciting groups. Their first full-length 2025 album, Roto, reached the top spot on the CKUA radio charts in its first week. Centered around the soulful sounds of the mbira, a traditional instrument from Zimbabwe, their music offers a fresh, energetic and spellbinding experience of cyclical arrangements, polyrhythm, layered vocals and improvisation. Seamlessly moving between re-interpretations of traditional repertoires and original compositions the band sings songs of family, heritage, celebrations and gatherings.

the-milk-carton-kids.name: The Milk Carton Kids
the-milk-carton-kids.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 8

Schedule is subject to change

The Milk Carton Kids have remained committed to a deceptively simple idea: music can help turn down the volume on a chaotic world and make room for what matters most. The latest release from the Grammy nominated duo, Lost Cause Lover Fool, is the clearest distillation of that idea yet. With roots-leaning arrangements and a deep trust in space, the album expands the duo’s signature minimalist sound while somehow making it even more focused.

the-point.name: The Point.
the-point.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Despite their influences ranging across the world, The Point. are a band that could only have started in Austin. Fusing psychedelia, Saharan guitar music, and cumbia with jam-band sensibilities, the trio creates a laid-back vibe that’s more than the sum of its parts. Their latest, Maldito Animal (Deluxe Edition), finds the band exploring new directions, the aural equivalent of a record store crate-digger showing off their collection, a panoply of taste culled into one source.

the-pairs.name: The Pairs
the-pairs.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Their sibling harmonies and familial banter quickly connect them to their audience. Whether The Pairs are playing an intimate house concert or a sold-out show, these classically trained vocalists make it their mission to use three-part harmony and honest storytelling as powerful tools that soften and connect us. “I first saw The Pairs at a folk festival do a rare thing - make a noisy, wandering crowd suddenly go quiet. You could tell right then they are something rare and one of the brightest new voices in Canadian folk.” (Tom Power, CBC)

the-womack-sisters.name: The Womack Sisters
the-womack-sisters.bio:
Concert: Thursday, August 6

Schedule is subject to change

The Womack Sisters, BG, Zeimani and Kucha Womack, are a new generation of soul royalty, renowned for their blend of R&B, soul, and gospel influences drawn from their rich family legacy. As granddaughters of the legendary Sam Cooke, daughters of renowned soul and R&B artists Womack & Womack, and nieces of Bobby Womack, they carry on the Womack family’s musical traditions, infusing their work with the soul and passion that has defined the family’s impact on music for generations.

thee-sacred-souls.name: Thee Sacred Souls
thee-sacred-souls.bio:
Concert: Thursday, August 6

Schedule is subject to change

The highly anticipated follow-up to their breakout 2022 self-titled debut, Thee Sacred Soul’s Got A Story To Tell, features 12 original songs, a soaring statement of exquisite craftsmanship from this young band from San Diego, whose own story grows bigger by the day. The group boasts millions of monthly listeners on Spotify, celebrity fans like SZA, Alicia Keys, and Kylie Jenner, an NPR Tiny Desk performance that burned the house down – and all in the first two years of the band’s existence.

tia-wood.name: Tia Wood
tia-wood.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Her powerful, soulful voice carries the spirit of her peoples’ songs that have echoed from her homelands since time immemorial, and the musical roots of her family tree run deep. Growing up in a home rich with music and culture, she began singing as soon as she could talk, inspired and nurtured by her parents, siblings, and her dual Plains Cree and Coast Salish heritage. Her 2025 release, “Sage My Soul”, “is Tia Wood’s most impressive vocal performance to date, her voice unabashed and soaring.” (Kelsey Adams, CBC)

trousdale.name: Trousdale
trousdale.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8- 9

Schedule is subject to change

Trousdale’s debut album, Out of My Mind, earned raves from the likes of the Boston Globe, Consequence, and Atwood Magazine for its powerful songwriting, immaculate harmonies, and ability to bridge gaps between country and indie pop. The group further honed those strengths on the road, finding deeper grooves and learning how to meticulously highlight each of their voices. Their 2025 album, Growing Pains, stares down all the weight of its title, a trio of strong, independent women facing the threats of loneliness, broken hearts, loss, and more, and converting it into transformative, uplifting music.

anna-muskego-nikamowin.name: Anna Muskego (Nikamowin)
anna-muskego-nikamowin.bio:
Anna Muskego is a mixed-blood Dene artist from Cold Lake First Nations who is passionate about sharing First Nations history and traditional crafts with learners of all ages. With experience teaching across various platforms, she currently serves as the Cultural Craftsperson at Fort Edmonton Park, where she brings ancestral knowledge to life through hands-on education and community engagement.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

carmen-miller-nikamowin.name: Carmen Miller (Nikamowin)
carmen-miller-nikamowin.bio:
Alberta-based Metis artisan, Carmen Miller, has been practising different art mediums for most of her life. She was born in Edmonton, grew up in a military family and moved from coast to coast in Canada. Carmen taught herself how to make simple beaded items at the age of eight and has been beading ever since. She has had some wonderful friends and teachers that have shared their knowledge of tanning hides, making birch bark baskets, tufting, and quillwork. 

She learned the art of moose hair and caribou hair tufting 32 years ago and her tufting’s are usually on wearable items such as moccasins, mittens, and jewelry and are combined with beadwork. She has made it her life’s work to learn and teach traditional Indigenous art forms. 

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

darla-daniels-nikamowin.name: Darla Daniels (Nikamowin)
darla-daniels-nikamowin.bio:
Darla Daniels is a dynamic Métis fiddler, singer-songwriter, actress, and championship Red River Jigger known for her energetic stage presence and powerful performances. Blending traditional Métis fiddle with roots, country, and Americana, she honours her heritage while creating a bold contemporary sound. Darla has performed at major festivals across North America and opened for acclaimed artists including Gary Allan and The Road Hammers. Her music has been featured on SiriusXM, APTN, CBC Radio, and Indigenous radio stations nationwide. An award-winning artist, Darla continues to captivate audiences through storytelling, fiddle music, and heartfelt original songs.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

lyndon-aginas-nikamowin.name: Lyndon Aginas (Nikamowin)
lyndon-aginas-nikamowin.bio:
Lyndon Aginas is a respected member of the Alexis Nakota Sioux Nation. Served on Council for his Nation and formerly as Casino President. With a professional background that includes work with Correctional Service Canada and the Ministry of Environment, Lyndon brings a wealth of leadership, knowledge, and lived experience to his work.

Deeply connected to the land and his culture, he is passionate about travelling, hunting, gathering traditional medicinal plants, and participating in spiritual ceremonies. Lyndon regularly shares his knowledge of Indigenous culture, history, and traditional teachings through presentations and engagement with institutions, governments, industries, and community organizations.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

metis-child-family-jiggers-nikamowin.name: Métis Child & Family Jiggers (Nikamowin)
metis-child-family-jiggers-nikamowin.bio:
The Métis Child & Family Jiggers celebrate Métis music and dance traditions through performances at festivals and community events. Performing together for over 22 years, the group began as children from the Edmonton area and continue dancing today out of their love for Métis fiddle music and culture. Their repertoire includes the Duck Dance, Reel of 4, Reel of 8, and the iconic Red River Jig. Now young adults in professional careers, they have been honoured with the City of Edmonton’s Salute to Excellence Arts & Culture Performance Award, accompanied by musicians Alfie and Byron Myhre.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

oskinakosiwin-indigenous-dance-group-nik.name: Oskinakosiwin Indigenous Dance Group (Nikamowin)
oskinakosiwin-indigenous-dance-group-nik.bio:
Oskinakosiwin Indigenous Dance Group was founded by acclaimed hoop dancer Lakota Tootoosis of Poundmaker Cree Nation, Saskatchewan. Oskinakosiwin Indigenous Dance Group is a newly formed collective dedicated to empowering and inspiring Indigenous dancers. Oskinakosiwin, meaning “new image” in Cree, reflects Lakota’s vision of supporting the next generation through culture, performance, and mentorship.

With over a decade of hoop dancing experience, Lakota has performed internationally in Taiwan, Alaska, and across North America. Through the storytelling art of hoop dance, audiences witness dynamic transformations representing animals, nature, and the interconnectedness of life. Lakota also shares his journey through motivational workshops and community engagement.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

celina-loyer-nikamowin.name: Celina Loyer (Nikamowin)
celina-loyer-nikamowin.bio:
Celina Loyer is a Métis artist who will be demonstrating the traditional art of finger weaving. The Métis sash, also known as the L’Assomption sash or Ceinture Fléchée (arrow sash), is a vibrant, finger-woven belt historically worn by Voyageurs and proudly embraced by their Métis descendants. Typically, three metres long, the sash is both a symbol of cultural identity and a practical tool rooted in the fur trade era.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

ekti-margaret-cardinal-nikamowin.name: Ekti Margaret Cardinal (Nikamowin)
ekti-margaret-cardinal-nikamowin.bio:
Ekti Margaret Cardinal is a respected Indigenous Knowledge Keeper from Saddle Lake First Nation, devoted to preserving and sharing the teachings of traditional Cree culture. Known for her exceptional craftsmanship as a Tipi and Tent Maker, Ekti carries the wisdom of her ancestors with deep reverence. She is also the longest-serving member of the Regional Environmental Action Committee (REAC), having held this role since 1990.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

goota-desmarais-nikamowin.name: Goota Desmarais (Nikamowin)
goota-desmarais-nikamowin.bio:
Goota Desmarais was raised in Cape Dorset, Nunavut, spending her winters in a modern Inuit settlement and summers in a traditional camp on the south shore of Baffin Island. Through her business, Inuit Connections, she has spent over 20 years educating audiences about Inuit culture, sharing personal stories that reflect the richness and resilience of life in the North.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

levi-wolfe-nikamowin.name: Levi Wolfe (Nikamowin)
levi-wolfe-nikamowin.bio:
Levi Wolfe is from Onion Lake Cree Nation and holds a Bachelor of Education from the University of Alberta. He is currently learning his Cree language to support beginner-level learners and is dedicated to sharing Indigenous hand games and their accompanying songs—tools that bring people from all four directions together in community and celebration.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

matt-hiltermann-nikamowin.name: Matt Hiltermann (Nikamowin)
matt-hiltermann-nikamowin.bio:
Matt Hiltermann is a Métis interpreter, artisan, and ethnohistorian with ancestral ties to the Pruden family and the historic Métis settlements of Edmonton, Fort Carlton, and Red River. Since 2008, he has contributed his knowledge and skills to the museum and heritage sector, working at institutions such as Heritage Park, Fort Edmonton Park, Lougheed House, and Fort Calgary.

Join us once again as the Edmonton Folk Music Festival celebrates First Nations, Métis, and Inuit artists through the Nikamowin Program and Indigenous-led programming across the festival site.

We acknowledge that the Edmonton Folk Music Festival takes place on Treaty 6 Territory, the traditional lands of the Cree, Dene, Blackfoot, Saulteaux, Nakota Sioux, and Métis peoples. We honour the enduring relationships Indigenous Peoples have with these lands and their ongoing contributions to our communities.

We invite festival-goers to gather in a spirit of respect, learning, and celebration as we share Indigenous stories, songs, cultures, and traditions through music, art, and community.

adam-baldwin.name: Adam Baldwin
adam-baldwin.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

With his sophomore full-length album, Concertos & Serenades, Adam Baldwin offers an east coast testimony that challenges the typical tourism marketing gloss. Through eight masterful yarns, the songwriter bears witness and pays tribute to a tradition of desperation, sinners and losers, perpetual failures, and down-and-out phantoms that haunt his home’s coastlines and back roads - without a passing judgement. Some of the tales happened, some didn’t, and most walk a tightrope between truth and fiction.

amble.name: Amble
amble.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Schedule is subject to change

Irish trio Amble harness a rare magic threaded into the fabric of their novelistic folk. The group of Robbie Cunningham, Ross McNerney, and Oisin McCaffrey have unassumingly gone from quitting their day jobs in rural Ireland to the doorstep of an international breakthrough buoyed by over 100 million streams and sold-out shows around the world. Their relatability, intimate storytelling, and authenticity marry seamlessly with no-frills acoustic instrumentation, calm-inducing soundscapes, and heavenly natural harmonies.

anna-tivel.name: Anna Tivel
anna-tivel.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Anna Tivel is a songwriter who is forever drawn to the quiet stories of ordinary life. Tivel has been likened to a short story writer and praised by NPR, Rolling Stone, Billboard, and others for her keen observations of the human condition. "Anna Tivel is just one of my absolute favourite living songwriters. Her writing on [Outsiders] is at the same level as Paul Simon when he wrote 'The Boxer' and 'American Tune.’” (Ann Power, NPR)

annie-the-caldwells.name: Annie & The Caldwells
annie-the-caldwells.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August, 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Annie & The Caldwells are a family that plays a powerful disco soul from West Point, Mississippi. Their latest album embraces the idea that “You don’t need to share the Caldwells’ faith to find something powerful and inspiring in [their lyrics], particularly given the current climate, which can easily incline you towards hopelessness; something steeped in tradition seems apropos right now. You should listen to Can’t Lose My (Soul) purely on musical terms. Moreover, it’s an album you might need.” (The Guardian)

arrested-development.name: Arrested Development
arrested-development.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Schedule is subject to change

Emerging as true trailblazers in the hip-hop scene, since 1991 Arrested Development stands as a cultural beacon of consciousness and empowerment worldwide. Their latest project, Adult Contemporary Hip-Hop, showcases their innovative spirit, unmatched lyricism, soulful storytelling, and profound awareness, exemplifying the unique artistry that only Arrested Development can provide.

asgeir.name: Ásgeir
asgeir.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

As one of Iceland’s most successful exports, singer-songwriter Ásgeir has always pushed the boundaries of his thoughtful brand of folk-pop. His debut album, Dýrð í dauðaþögn, became the best-selling debut in Icelandic history, leading to the English-language version, ‘In The Silence’ in 2014.  He continued to refine his sound with albums Afterglow, Bury The Moon, and Time On My Hands, earning praise from press and radio worldwide, including The Independent, MOJO, NME, Clash Magazine, BBC 6 Music and Radio X.

bella-white.name: Bella White
bella-white.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Bella White is a truly one-of-a-kind musical talent: a finespun breed of country-folk both fresh in perspective and wholly steeped in bluegrass tradition. “Her coos and the breaks in her voice swell empathy and commiseration in the audience, and whether you’re a young adult going through the same struggles of finding where you should to be and with whom, or an older person reminiscing on that era in your own life, the music of Bella White finds deep appeal.” (Saving Country Music)

bombino.name: Bombino
bombino.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Grammy-nominated Nigerien Tuareg guitar hero Bombino, hailed by The New York Times as the "Sultan of Shred", continues to redefine desert blues and rock for a global audience. Over the years, Bombino has collaborated with acclaimed producers such as Dan Auerbach (The Black Keys) on Nomad and David Longstreth (Dirty Projectors) on Azel, earning praise from icons including Keith Richards, Stevie Wonder, Robert Plant, and Arcade Fire.

brianna-lizotte.name: Brianna Lizotte
brianna-lizotte.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Brianna Lizotte is a JUNO nominated fiddle player from Edmonton. She grew up in a household where music was constantly played, whether it was from home videos or kitchen parties. Brianna’s two albums, Scratch ‘Em, and her newest project, Winston & I, have received radio play on CBC Radio, CJSR, CFWE, and appeared on CKUA’s Top 30 chart. She can also be found teaching Metis history through music and dance workshops to students across the province.

buffalo-traffic-jam.name: Buffalo Traffic Jam
buffalo-traffic-jam.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Schedule is subject to change

Buffalo Traffic Jam is a band born in the heart of Bozeman where its members first crossed paths as students at Montana State University. United by a love for the raw beauty of acoustic sound, they began crafting music that evokes wide-open landscapes and the emotions that live quietly beneath the surface of everyday life. The result was a self-titled debut EP followed by singles, “Rescue Me” and “Forgot Your Roots,” the latter of which saw the band gain their first bit of online momentum, leading to explosive growth in the past year.

buyepongo.name: Buyepongo
buyepongo.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Buyepongo, a band known for their infectious Buyangu sound, has become a well-known name in the Los Angeles music scene. Their music is a riotous fusion of influences, seamlessly blending cumbia, hip-hop, punk, funk, and jazz into a deliriously infectious tropical mix that spans the rich tapestry of Latin American culture. This dynamic ensemble has shared the stage with musical heavyweights like Miguel, Warren G, Common, Ozomatli, Celso Piña, Sister Nancy, Sharon Jones and the Dap Kings, and Antibalas.

cat-power.name: Cat Power
cat-power.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Schedule is subject to change

Cat Power’s seventh studio album, The Greatest proved to be an immediate landmark in her already acclaimed career upon its 2006 release. Named by Rolling Stone at #6 on its “Top 50 Albums of 2006” as well #26 on its “100 Best Albums of the 2000s,” the collection was awarded with 2006’s prestigious Shortlist Music Prize, making history as the first album by a female artist to be accorded that honour.  The festival is honoured to celebrate the 20th year of one of Cat Power’s most iconic albums.

corb-lund.name: Corb Lund
corb-lund.bio:
Concert: Thursday, August 6

Schedule is subject to change

Corb Lund has received multiple CCMA, JUNO, and international award nominations and wins. A rural Albertan hailing from the foothills of the Rocky Mountains with a long family lineage of ranchers and rodeo people, Lund is about as authentic as they come. Embracing both his Western heritage and his indie rock past through his music, he has been known to filter a range of cowboy themes past and present through his unique lens.

courtney-marie-andrews.name: Courtney Marie Andrews
courtney-marie-andrews.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

From her 2016 breakthrough album, Honest Life, to 2020’s Grammy-nominated, Old Flowers, Courtney Marie Andrews has been celebrated as an artist who challenges herself, and who finds new interplays of Folk and Americana. According to Andrews, her latest release, Valentine, is “a record in pursuit of love”. But love, it turns out, “is a lot more than I gave it credit for,” she explains. “It’s built over years, it’s built with trust, with changes, it becomes something new and unrecognizable, the deeper you go.”

dk-harrell.name: D.K. Harrell
dk-harrell.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Guitarist, vocalist and songwriter D.K. Harrell, one of the most formidable young stars of the current blues resurgence, sounds like no one else on the scene today. With his stinging, single-note string bending, deep soul vocals, and attention-grabbing original songs, Harrell is already in a league of his own. “With every exuberant turn of phrase, and each sharp guitar lick, the Louisiana born showman displays a pure joy in performing that’s hard to resist. …Ultimately, Harrell’s greatest talent is just being his own larger-than-life self.” (No Depression)

daby-toure.name: Daby Touré
daby-toure.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Whether he appears solo or with his full band, Daby Touré never fails to astound audiences with his ethereal voice, masterful guitar work and catchy original songs of “global soul-tinged pop.” (BBC) Most recently, Daby composed and recorded the soundtrack for the 2025 documentary, La Sang et La Boue (Of Blood and Mud), about the child miners in Congo who mine for coltan, a sought after mineral used in our cell phones. The music is as beautiful as is the scenery where the film was shot, accompanying an incredibly hard and important story.

dailey-vincent.name: Dailey & Vincent
dailey-vincent.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Schedule is subject to change

Grand Ole Opry members and Grammy nominated, Dailey & Vincent, are among the most respected and celebrated duos in American roots music. With unmatched harmonies, masterful musicianship, and a signature blend of bluegrass, country, and gospel, Jamie Dailey and Darrin Vincent have built a career defined by excellence, authenticity, and heart. In 2026, they reached a major milestone with “Ruby,” a multi-week No. 1 song on the Bluegrass Today chart.

damien-okane-ron-block.name: Damien O'Kane & Ron Block
damien-okane-ron-block.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

What do you get when an American bluegrass banjo and an Irish tenor banjo join forces? In the case of Coleraine’s Damien O’Kane and California’s Ron Block – fireworks. Widely regarded as two of the greatest exponents of the banjo, they have collaborated to create arguably their own genre, Banjophony, something never heard before that is subtle, awe-inspiring, energetic and majestic.

dana-sipos.name: Dana Sipos
dana-sipos.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Dana Sipos coaxes silkworms out of their cocoons and spins them into song. She inhabited the far north before going nomad and her captivatingly nuanced songs are filled with a wild wind and a haunting surrealism. She now resides among the old growth of Vancouver Island. A consummate performer, Sipos has brought her unique brand of cosmic folk to living rooms, concert halls and festival stages across North America, Europe, Australia and New Zealand.

djekady-feat-balla-kouyate-mike-block.name: Djékady feat. Balla Kouyaté & Mike Block
djekady-feat-balla-kouyate-mike-block.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Balla Kouyaté and Mike Block met through their work with the Silkroad Ensemble, and have found a simpatico relationship with each other. “Balla’s joy and unbridled enthusiasm is infectious,” Block says. “There is so much trust between us,” offers Kouyaté. “We treat each other like brothers.” Djékady is a Mandinka word with the meaning, “coming together is good.” Every song is a trust fall, with each musician leaping higher and higher knowing their bandmates will catch them every time.

djely-tapa.name: Djely Tapa
djely-tapa.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8

Schedule is subject to change

With Dankoraba, her 2025 JUNO award winning album, Djely Tapa pursues her artistic vision at the crossroads of Malian and Western cultures, past, present and future. Originally from the Kayes region of western Mali, Tapa shares the wise words and vocal art of the griots, emblematic figures of ancient and contemporary Malian society, whose origins date back to the 13th-century Mandingo Empire. Her repertoire blends Mandingo traditions, desert blues and electronic sounds.

dove-ellis.name: Dove Ellis
dove-ellis.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 8

Schedule is subject to change

Cutting his teeth in Manchester's flourishing live scene, Dove Ellis has been spreading word-of-mouth after notable performances, earning him an opening spot on Geese’s 2025 American tour. On his debut album, Blizzard, “Ellis’s remarkable vocals can settle into a dreamy falsetto so fragile he could be dancing on a pin, and then suddenly perform a handbrake turn into intensity, even anger. The way the arrangements…dart around his voice in ornate little counter-melodies recalls fellow Irishman Van Morrison, and recent single, “To the Sandals” noticeably nods to Joan Armatrading’s “Love and Affection”.” (The Guardian)

dug.name: DUG
dug.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

The duo’s music stems from roots in musical traditions spanning both sides of the pond. In one breath, echoing the great American folk troubadours, and in another, comfortably channeling the elder statesmen of Irish folk. This is no accident. DUG have a shared musical heritage, with members having been born in America and Scotland before arriving in Ireland.

fantastic-cat.name: Fantastic Cat
fantastic-cat.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Individually, each member of Fantastic Cat boasts their own impressive résumé along with a litany of critical acclaim. Collectively, though, the four transcend their respective roots, emerging as a velvet-clad rock and roll cooperative far greater than the sum of its parts. They say some cats are born fantastic; others have fantasy thrust upon them. These guys are somewhere in the middle.

gwenifer-raymond.name: Gwenifer Raymond
gwenifer-raymond.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Wales born instrumentalist Gwenifer Raymond is a celebrated champion of the finger-style guitar. She has drawn international acclaim for her repurposing of Mississippi blues and John Fahey’s intricate Americana to embody her roots in rural South Wales. The Guardian has described her as a “profound talent” whilst The Observer has praised her “awe-inspiring technique and intense musicality”, and Uncut Magazine has championed her “fast-developing talents as a composer of eerie menace.”

hurray-for-the-riff-raff.name: Hurray for the Riff Raff
hurray-for-the-riff-raff.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 8

Schedule is subject to change

 

Hurray for the Riff Raff founder, Alynda Segarra, has been something of a modern Huck Finn, an itinerant traveler whose adventures prompt art that reminds us there are always other ways to live. Born in the Bronx and of Puerto Rican heritage, Segarra was raised by a blue-collar aunt and uncle, where they were radicalized before they were a teenager, baptized in the anti-war movement and galvanized in New York’s punk haunts and queer spaces. They steadily rose from house shows to a major label, where Segarra became a pan-everything fixture of the modern folk movement.

jake-xerxes-fussell.name: Jake Xerxes Fussell
jake-xerxes-fussell.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Reared in Georgia and settled in North Carolina, Jake Xerxes Fussell has established himself as a devoted listener and contemplative interpreter of a vast array of folk songs, lovingly sourced from a personal store of favourites. On his latest album, When I’m Called, his first as a parent, Fussell returns to a well of music that holds lifelong sentimental meaning, loosely contemplating the passage of time and the procession of life’s unexpected offerings.

jeffrey-martin.name: Jeffrey Martin
jeffrey-martin.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Jeffrey Martin has been reaching for quiet and stories since he was a kid hiding under the covers with a Discman. He wrote his way through a writing degree, a teaching degree, and years of high school English classes filled with unruly brilliance, all while making records and playing shows whenever he could. Eventually, he left teaching to follow the same dream he encouraged his students to chase. After releasing, Thank God We Left the Garden, he recorded, Alive, July 25, 2025, a no-edits live album captured in Portland with Sam Weber.

jeremie-albino.name: Jeremie Albino
jeremie-albino.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Jeremie Albino’s songs are grounded in the gritty storytelling of classic country music, propelled by the rhythms of old-school R&B, played with the wild abandon of early rock ’n’ roll, and sung with the deep feeling of southern soul. Thanks to his sweaty, livewire concerts, he has been steadily growing his audience from a few passersby to packed houses around Canada and the United States.

joe-nolan.name: Joe Nolan
joe-nolan.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

A virtuoso guitarist, elusive poet, and musical chameleon, Joe Nolan was raised in the rural community of Sturgeon County, and has always lived on the outskirts, being a hard one to pin down. Breaking genres and defying labels, he defines his sound as "...my caravan diaries." Hailed by The Globe and Mail as "an Americana wunderkind to watch out for," Joe Nolan is proving that praise is well-deserved with his newly released album, Luv in the New World, produced by Grammy Award-winner Tyler Chester.

joel-plaskett-emergency.name: Joel Plaskett Emergency
joel-plaskett-emergency.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

For over three decades, Joel Plaskett has proven himself a spinning wheel of reinvention. The Nova Scotia songwriter's eclectic body of work reveals a restless and playful spirit, always transforming and expanding. His revered career, saw the release of, Songs From The Gang – A Celebration of Joel Plaskett – a deluxe covers album featuring the music of Joel Plaskett performed by Arkells, Sloan, Jill Barber, The Sheepdogs, Julian Taylor, Jenn Grant, Bahamas, City and Colour, Rose Cousins, Frank Turner, and many more.

john-r-miller.name: John R. Miller
john-r-miller.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

John R. Miller grew up in Hedgesville, a small town in eastern West Virginia where the mountains meet I-81. Crafting his own blend of laid-back country, blues and folk, he has performed music in nearly all 50 states, Canada, Ireland, the UK, Japan, and much of Europe. His songs have been performed and recorded by several notable artists, including Tyler Childers and David F. Bello. John’s latest album, The Trouble You Follow, “...is about taking all the wrong forks in the road and never being dealt a lucky draw, but deciding to be content and live your life anyway, the mess be damned.” (Saving Country Music)

julian-taylor.name: Julian Taylor
julian-taylor.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Award-winning, Toronto-based singer-songwriter Julian Taylor has been part of the musical fabric and landscape in Canada for two decades. He enjoyed a breakthrough year in 2020, when his second solo acoustic album, The Ridge, earned more than five million plays on Spotify, and praise from press worldwide. In addition to winning “Solo Artist of the Year” at the Canadian Folk Music Awards, The Ridge was also nominated for two JUNO Awards, the Polaris Prize Long List, and a Summer Solstice Indigenous Award.

kate-rusby.name: Kate Rusby
kate-rusby.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

For over three decades, Kate Rusby has stood as one of the most influential and beloved figures in British folk music. Frequently described as “...a superstar of the British acoustic scene” (The Telegraph), and affectionately hailed as “Folk Royalty” (The Daily), she has carved a singular place in the UK’s musical landscape. Among her many accolades, she has also been awarded an impressive three Honorary Doctorates from three separate universities, all for her service to music, the arts and the preservation of folk song.

la-deferlance.name: La Déferlance
la-deferlance.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

La Déferlance offers a refreshing and brightly coloured take on traditional music. Alongside imaginative arrangements of lively tunes and traditional songs, the band’s repertoire features many original compositions. Their energetic and polished performances are a testament to its members' years of varied musical experience, notably in jazz and classical music. Recipient of numerous nominations, the group now travels around the globe to present its new show, Âmes des bois, created from its second album released in 2025.

leif-vollebekk.name: Leif Vollebekk
leif-vollebekk.bio:
Concert: Saturday, August 8

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Leif Vollebekk is an acclaimed singer-songwriter originally from Ottawa who now resides in Montreal. Vollebekk has sold-out headlining tours across North America, Europe and Australia. His 2017 release, Twin Solitude, was a breakthrough album, landing a spot on the Polaris Music Prize shortlist, a JUNO nomination and gathering over 60 million streams. “[His] songs […] are pensive and restless, blossoming with stream-of-consciousness verses that bear the marks of long nights spent alone on the road.” (The New York Times)

madalitso-band.name: Madalitso Band
madalitso-band.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

A dynamic musical duo from Lilongwe, Malawi who are known for their infectious energy and distinctive sound that is created using homemade instruments, Madalitso Band’s music blends Malawian traditions with a raw, rhythmic intensity that captivates audiences worldwide. Their name, “Madalitso,” meaning “blessings,” reflects both their uplifting spirit and the remarkable journey that has taken them from the streets of Malawi to global stages. What began as street performances to survive has evolved into an international touring career, for Yobu Maligwa and Yosefe Kalekeni.

mariel-buckley.name: Mariel Buckley
mariel-buckley.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 8

Schedule is subject to change

Unapologetically genuine, wry and soft-hearted, Mariel Buckley has planted her flag firmly as a songwriter who’s in it for the long haul. Raised in the heart of Canada’s rodeo and country music scenes, Buckley was destined to challenge the boundaries around her, both in life and in song. Written largely during a reflective period in Edmonton and finished in Nashville, her latest album, Strange Trip Ahead, is a meditation on the push and pull of creative life — the exhaustion and exhilaration of pursuing meaning through art.

mia-kelly.name: Mia Kelly
mia-kelly.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 7 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Mia Kelly is an award-winning singer-songwriter from Gatineau, Quebec, making her mark on the Canadian folk music scene with a distinctive blend of folk, rock, and blues. Since releasing her 2022 debut album, Garden Through The War, she has been recognized as a poetic lyricist with a powerful and versatile voice. Known for her ability to weave the stories of those around her into her music, Kelly is an imaginative storyteller who channels raw, vulnerable emotion through expertly crafted songs.

nathaniel-rateliff.name: Nathaniel Rateliff
nathaniel-rateliff.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Schedule is subject to change

Raised in Hermann, Missouri, Nathaniel Rateliff began his music career playing drums in his family’s church band. “Whether he’s grinding out old-school soul with the Night Sweats or flying solo, he’s obsessed with the idea of authenticity: the idea that a song isn’t a song unless it can be convincingly played alone on an acoustic guitar.” (Pitchfork) Showcasing his true craftsmanship as a songwriter first, it is an honour to feature a solo Rateliff – the perfect punctuation to the 2026 festival.

nick-mulvey.name: Nick Mulvey
nick-mulvey.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

His songs don’t shout for attention, yet they demand it, with the poetic weight of Leonard Cohen, the introspective fragility of Nick Drake, and the hypnotic, polyrhythmic pulse of West African guitar masters like Ali Farka Touré. He studied music and art in Havana, Cuba, and was an integral part of the Mercury Award-nominated Portico Quartet, before launching his solo career. His album, First Mind, was a remarkable debut that left a lasting impression on all who listened.

ocie-elliott.name: Ocie Elliott
ocie-elliott.bio:
Concert: Sunday, August 9

Days of Sessions: Aug 8 - 9

Schedule is subject to change

Ocie Elliott is the JUNO nominated musical duo of Jon Middleton and Sierra Lundy. The two have come a long way in a short time since a chance encounter in a Salt Spring Island café led to a spark, collaboration, and their ultimate partnership in music. The duo pens tunes that feel lived-in. You can hear their memories, moments, and emotions in the dusty acoustic guitars, the sparse production, and the graceful harmonies between Middleton and Lundy. The duo has been aptly described by one reviewer as “...the aural equivalent of indigo painted skies and the first summer stars."

of-monsters-and-men.name: Of Monsters and Men
of-monsters-and-men.bio:
Concert: Friday, August 7

Schedule is subject to change

Of Monsters and Men went from the talk of Iceland to the top of the global conversation on their 2011 debut, My Head Is An Animal, earning them performances at festivals such as Coachella, Lollapalooza, Glastonbury, and beyond. Their 2025 album, All is Love and Pain in the Mouse Parade, “shows off an emotional depth and darkness not necessarily associated with the ‘Little Talks’ party starters. It’s more introspective, the band say, and the vocals of Nanna Hilmarsdóttir allow their message to hit with a proper punch.” (Rolling Stone)
